basics.name: Dr. Jayden L. Macklin-Cordes
basics.label: Quantitative historical linguist
basics.summary:
I am a quantitative historical linguist investigating how language structures change over
deep time, and what their evolution reveals about cultural evolution and human cognition.
I employ a primarily Bayesian toolkit: phylogenetic tree inference, phylogenetic
comparative methods, ancestral state reconstruction, and phylogeographic regression.
My PhD and subsequent published works develop phylogenetic methods for phonology,
particularly phonotactics, with a regional focus on languages of Australia. In parallel,
I work on the evolution of conceptual categories (nominal classification, case semantics,
number), with work extending to broader Sahul (Australia and New Guinea), Tibeto-Burman,
and worldwide language samples. Most recently, I have turned attention to the design of
agentic LLM pipelines for large-scale extraction of linguistic data from published grammars.

work.summary: School of Creative Industries, Humanities and Social Sciences

work.company: CNRS, Université Lumière Lyon 2, Lyon, France
highlights: EVOGRAM: The role of linguistic and non-linguistic factors in the evolution of nominal classification system

courses: Minor: Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander studies

skills.name: Statistical methods
keywords: Bayesian inference and multilevel modelling (brms), Bayesian phylogenetic tree inference (BEAST), phylogenetic comparative methods, ancestral state reconstruction, phylogeographic regression (BayesTraits, R).

skills.name: Programming
keywords: R (highly proficient, including the tidyverse, Bioconductor suite, phylogenetic packages [ape, phytools], geospatial packages [sf]), Python, Javascript, LaTeX.

skills.name: Research automation
keywords: Design of agentic LLM pipelines for large-scale extraction of linguistic data (e.g. interlinear glossed text) from published grammars. The agent consults structured knowledge bases (IGT conventions, IPA, observations accumulated from prior grammars), then iteratively drafts, self-critiques, and refines its output while recording grammar-specific idiosyncrasies for later use. A prototype pipeline benchmarked against manual annotation on an Australian grammar sample achieved 99% precision, 99% recall.

skills.name: Data visualisation
keywords: ggplot2, ggmap, ggtree. Interactive apps and mapping (Shiny, leaflet).

skills.name: Open science
keywords: Git (https://github.com/JaydenM-C), Zenodo/OSF (e.g. https://zenodo.org/records/7257104). Reproducibility tools (renv, Docker).

portfolio.name: phonfreq
portfolio.description: A package for visualising phoneme frequency distributions in Australian languages.

portfolio.name: CLtemplate, DeGruytertemplate, LDCtemplate, UQpresentation
portfolio.description: A series of packages providing Rmarkdown templates for authoring Computational Linguistics articles, De Gruyter articles and books, Language Dynamics and Change articles and University of Queensland-branded slideshow presentations. All at https://github.com/JaydenM-C/[name].

portfolio.name: JaydenM-C/PN_treebuilding
portfolio.description: A repository of code (R, C, TeX) for inferring phylogenetic trees from phonotactic and lexical data.

portfolio.name: JaydenM-C/chromatography
portfolio.description: Codebase for Chromatography (https://chromatography.pages.dev/). A web-based desktop app for crafting colour palettes from photographs.

portfolio.name: JaydenM-C/sc-alchemist
portfolio.description: Repository of code (Python) implementing MCMC optimisation for AFL fantasy team selection, with user-defined constraint functions.

portfolio.name: JaydenM-C/AusErdle
portfolio.description: Codebase for the AusErdle app, a Wordle spinoff using Australian English phonemic transcriptions.
